title: Crepidomanes schmidianum
taxonomy: Crepidomanes schmidianum is classified within the family Hymenophyllaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the filmy ferns. This species specifically belongs to the genus Crepidomanes, which consists of various species of epiphytic and terrestrial filmy ferns. Within the genus Crepidomanes, Crepidomanes schmidianum is considered a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and geographical distribution. The family Hymenophyllaceae is further classified within the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. As a member of the Pteridophyta division, Crepidomanes schmidianum is one of the vascular plants that reproduce via spores.

intro: Crepidomanes schmidianum, also known as Schmidia, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, a type of epiphytic fern that grows on other plants or trees. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its extracts have been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Crepidomanes schmidianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Crepidomanes schmidianum is often made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
